title: Prefer server-side enforcement
description: Client checks can guide UX, but security decisions must be enforced on the trusted side of the boundary.
body: Use when a requested change relies on hidden buttons, disabled fields, client route guards, local storage flags, mobile app checks, or generated SDK assumptions. Do not use to remove useful client-side affordances; keep them as secondary hints. Actions: locate the server, worker, database policy, or trusted service that owns the decision and add enforcement there before touching UI-only behavior. Validation signals include direct API or service tests that bypass the client and still receive deny results. Risk boundary: if no trusted enforcement point exists, stop at a finding or design note and escalate rather than claiming the change is secure.
